content_of_post_to_answer: How do university events contribute to the overall personality development of students?
answer: University events matter less because they look good on a resume and more because they expose you to situations with no syllabus: resolving disagreements, speaking when you're unsure, and working with people you didn't choose. Those moments quietly shape judgment. I explore this idea in a bit more depth on my Quora profile if it's of interest. Personality often develops through repeated responsibility, not isolated achievement.
